name: Research Alliance Corp III
business: Research Alliance Corp III is a blank check company formed to effect a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, or similar business combination with one or more businesses. It has no operating history and has not generated any revenues to date. The company intends to focus on the healthcare industry, specifically targeting companies in drug development and commercialization, diagnostic, and healthcare technology and service sectors.

exposures.sentence: In particular, we are targeting companies in the drug development and commercialization, diagnostic and healthcare technology and service sectors where our management has extensive investment experience.

In particular, we are targeting companies in the drug development and commercialization, diagnostic and healthcare technology and service sectors where our management has extensive investment experience.

catalysts.sentence: We will have up to 24 months from the closing of this offering to consummate an initial business combination.

tamClaims.claim: Total U.S. national health expenditures grew to $5.3 trillion in 2024 and accounted for 18% of U.S Gross Domestic Product.
tamClaims.sentence: Overall, total U.S. national health expenditures grew to $5.3 trillion in 2024 and accounted for 18% of U.S Gross Domestic Product, and Medicare and Medicaid Services grew to represent 21% and 18%, respectively of total national health expenditures in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: 216 M&A transactions totaling $534.0 billion since 2020 in the biotechnology sector.
tamClaims.sentence: In addition to these industry-wide dynamics in healthcare, specifically within biotechnology sector, the past decade has seen a shift in the research and development strategy of the large pharma companies to external innovation through licensing partnerships and strategic acquisitions, resulting in 216 M&A transactions totaling $534.0 billion since 2020 according to Evaluate.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately $300.0 billion in annual revenue at risk from loss of exclusivity in 2026-2031 period across large pharma companies.
tamClaims.sentence: Across large pharma companies there is approximately $300.0 billion in annual revenue at risk from loss of exclusivity in 2026-2031 period, making the imperative to acquire greater than ever.

tamClaims.claim: Only 47 biotechnology companies have gone public via IPO since 2022 in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Only 47 biotechnology companies have gone public via IPO since 2022 in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: More than 20,000 biotechnology companies globally in February 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Pitchbook, in February 2026, there were estimated to be more than 20,000 biotechnology companies globally, only a fraction of which are publicly traded.
